title: Add bridge_enabled field to /api/state response
objective: Expose whether the model bridge kill switch is currently on or off via the /api/state endpoint so the dashboard header badge can show BRIDGE ON or BRIDGE OFF accurately.
inputs: core/model_bridge.py line 11: ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE = True (after BRIDGE_ENABLE_001 is applied)
The /api/state endpoint returns the state dict from _read_state()
deliverables: In _read_state(), import ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE from core.model_bridge at the top of the file (or inline import inside the function if preferred)
Add bridge_enabled: bool to the returned state dict
If the import fails for any reason, default bridge_enabled to False (don't crash)
acceptance_criteria: /api/state JSON response contains bridge_enabled key with value true or false
Value reflects the actual ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE constant in core/model_bridge.py
No crash if import fails — graceful fallback to False
verification: curl http://localhost:5000/api/state | python -m json.tool | findstr bridge_enabled
